     Never hand-edit; stale anchors are rebuilt, not fixed. -->

# Code Anchors: Portable Tooling

- `libs/portable-tools/src/bundle.ts` — the shared bundling primitive: compiles one entry point into a self-contained Node-targeted bundle, inlining every non-builtin import; the working dir is pinned so the bytes (and their hash) are cwd-independent.
- `libs/portable-tools/src/build-bundles.ts` — builds every entry point (validator, atlas generator, and the hub diff-derivation tool) into self-contained bundles; the nx `bundle` target.
- `libs/portable-tools/src/validate-concepts.ts` — the authoritative concept validator (concept pages and anchor sidecars), bundled unchanged for the hub.
- `libs/portable-tools/src/generate-atlas.ts` — the authoritative atlas generator (incl. `--check` sync mode), bundled unchanged for the hub.
- `libs/portable-tools/src/derive-entry-diff.ts` — the hub diff-derivation tool: the first bundled tool to consult the workspace resolver at run time, resolving an entry's recorded range to per-repo diffs inside the member checkouts.
- `libs/portable-tools/src/vendor-bundle.ts` — the one build + fingerprint-pin + copy-into-hub step; takes the destination as an argument and hard-codes no hub path.
- `libs/portable-tools/src/parity.ts` — parity primitives: the fingerprint check (catches a vendored bundle stale against its source) and the executed source-vs-bundle diff over the corpus (findings, exit codes, atlas bytes), naming any divergence.
- `libs/portable-tools/src/parity.spec.ts` — the required parity gate, wired into the packaging project's `test` target.
- `libs/portable-tools/bundle-fingerprint.json` — the committed pin the vendor step writes and the parity spec asserts against a fresh build; now pins three bundles.
- `libs/portable-tools/corpus/` — the representative corpus exercising every validator finding category and non-trivial atlas clustering.
- `libs/portable-tools/project.json` — the buildable-lib targets (bundle, vendor, test).
- `libs/workspace/src/resolve.ts` — reports the hub's vendored portable-tools location as part of the resolved workspace context, and is the resolver the diff-derivation tool consults.
- `.claude/commands/nxs.distill.md` — the drain step that selects the hub bundle vs the in-repo invocation by workspace role.
- `docs/features/multi-repo-workspaces/hub-tooling-install.md` — the one-time hub placement guide for the vendored tooling.
